calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 3) And Req. For Production Of Documents (Set 1) And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Mar-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance; PLAINTIFF ARASH MOUSSAVIAN's Motion To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 3) And Req. For Production Of Documents (Set 1) And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction IS OFF CALENDAR. Judge Pro tem: Katherine Huibonhoa; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany; Not Reported.=302/JPT.

Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set 3 And Request For Production Of Documents Set 1 And An Award For Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set 3 And Request For Production Of Documents Set 1 And An Award For Monetary Sanction IS A DUPLICATE ENTRY

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 3) And Req. For Production Of Documents (Set 1) And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Continued To Mar-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance, The Court adopted it's tentative ruling; PLAINTIFF ARASH MOUSSAVIAN'S MOTION To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 3) And Req. For Production Of Documents (Set 1) And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction is denied in its entirety. The motion seeks to compel further responses to three special interrogatories (Nos. 15, 17 and 19 of Set 3, each of which seeks the identification of documents supporting positions), and six requests for production (Nos. 2, 4, 7, 8, 9 and 11). The motion papers are excessively prolix, and do not clearly identify the relief desired, or the need for it. This court also doubts that plaintiff complied with its obligation to meet and confer in good faith in an effort to finally resolve the dispute without court intervention. The only indication of a meet and confer effort after plaintiff received the last formal responses from defendant was a letter dated January 16, 2017, which does not indicate any willingness to discuss or compromise, but instead threatens to file a motion 3-4 days later if defendant did not provide further supplemental responses curing what plaintiff perceived as deficiencies in defendant's responses. The supplemental document response (Exh. Q to the declaration in support, beginning with p. 160 of the electronic submission) reveals that as to each document request, defendant said that he had conducted a reasonable and diligent search and had produced all responsive documents in his custody. The opposition memorandum also states that defendant advised plaintiff that he had nothing further to produce in mid-December. Memo. at 2. A court cannot order production or identification of documents or things that do not exist. Prevailing party to prepare Order. Judge Pro tem: Charles R. Ragan; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany; Not Reported.=302/jpt
***MATTER IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 10 2017 AT 9:00AM IN DEPT. 302 PER JPT, AFTER EMAILING THE COURT ON FEB, 21 2017.

Off Calendar Feb-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff ARASH MOUSSAUVIAN, ESQ., Tel No. 415-695-4545, appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court finds that Plaintiff did not properly notify opposing parties of his intent to appear at hearing to contest the Court's tentative ruling. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ARASH MOUSSAVIAN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. All of the affirmative defenses are adequately pled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF ARASH MOUSSAVIAN'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT BRENT BORGSTEDE'S ANSWER TO COMPLAINT - The general demurrer to the answer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. All of the affirmative defenses are totally conclusory and defendant fails to plead ultimate facts. See FPI Dev., Inc. v. Nakashima, (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
